mappings.rationaleAI: Dance as aesthetic body movement spanning ceremonial, sacred, competitive, and recreational contexts addresses the art of dance (A), its nature and origins (A.1), diverse classifications (A.3), primitive, folk, and popular dance (D), and the history of dance (E).
Theatre as collaborative performing art using gesture, song, and dance connects to the integration of dance with other arts in the theatre (A.5).
Performing arts including theatre, dance, and music address the art of dance broadly (A) and dance's integration with other performing arts (A.5).
Opera incorporating dance connects to the integration of dance with other arts (A.5) and the integration of ballet with opera (B.2).
Michael Jackson's popularization of the moonwalk and visual performance aesthetics in popular music addresses popular dance (D).
Music as the typical accompaniment for dance connects to the integration of dance with other arts (A.5).
Entertainment including dance as audience activity connects to popular dance (D).
Film connects to the integration of dance in motion pictures (A.5).
Chaplin's physical comedy and movement-based performance connect to dance in motion pictures (A.5).
France as the birthplace of classical ballet connects to the history of ballet (B.4) and the dance of Western peoples (E.1).
Romanticism's influence on ballet composition and expression addresses the history of ballet (B.4) and Western dance history (E.1).
Classical music's orchestral accompaniment for ballet addresses the integration of ballet with other arts (B.2).
Ancient Greece's theatrical and ceremonial dance traditions connect to the dance of Western peoples (E.1).
Spain's flamenco and other regional dance traditions connect to folk and popular dance (D).
Pop music's emphasis on danceability connects to popular dance (D).
Europe's cultural traditions encompassing classical ballet and folk dance address the dance of Western peoples (E.1).
